# Boldify

> Free utility tool that converts text to Unicode-styled characters (bold, italic, strikethrough, underline) for LinkedIn posts. Multi-language (EN/FR/ES/PT/DE/TR/PL). Open source.

## Available Tools

- MCP server: https://api.boldify.net/mcp
  - convert: transform HTML or Markdown to Unicode-formatted text
  - syntaxes: read formatting reference at boldify://syntaxes/markdown and boldify://syntaxes/html

## Citable Pages

- [Home](https://boldify.net/en): tool overview and live editor
- [How It Works](https://boldify.net/en/how-it-works): 4-step process explaining the Unicode mechanism
- [Help / FAQ](https://boldify.net/en/help): common issues, mobile rendering, screen reader notes
- [About](https://boldify.net/en/about): creator and project background
- [MCP](https://boldify.net/en/mcp): integration guide for Claude Desktop and other MCP clients

## How It Works (full)

Boldify uses Unicode mathematical alphanumeric characters (Unicode block U+1D400–U+1D7FF) to simulate bold, italic, and other typographic styles. These characters are part of the Unicode standard and render natively on LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and most modern platforms without HTML or markdown support.

### Formatting Process

1. User types or pastes text into the web editor (powered by Quill.js)
2. User selects text and applies a style (Bold, Italic, Strikethrough, etc.) via the toolbar
3. The handler maps each ASCII character to its Unicode mathematical equivalent
4. The styled text is inserted back into the editor and shown in the live LinkedIn preview card
5. User clicks "Copy" to copy the Unicode text to clipboard and paste it into LinkedIn

### Supported Styles

- **Bold** — Unicode Mathematical Bold (𝗕𝗼𝗹𝗱)
- **Italic** — Unicode Mathematical Italic (𝘐𝘵𝘢𝘭𝘪𝘤)
- **Strikethrough** — Unicode combining strikethrough (S̶t̶r̶i̶k̶e̶)
- **Underline** — Unicode combining underline
- **Overline** — Unicode combining overline
- **Bullet lists** — Unicode bullet characters
- **Numbered lists** — formatted numbered sequences

### MCP Integration

The Boldify MCP (Model Context Protocol) server at https://api.boldify.net/mcp enables AI assistants to format text directly without opening the web app. The server exposes:

- `convert` tool: accepts HTML or Markdown input, returns Unicode-formatted text
- `boldify://syntaxes/markdown` resource: Markdown formatting reference
- `boldify://syntaxes/html` resource: HTML formatting reference

Compatible with Claude Desktop and any standard MCP client.

## Help / FAQ (full)

### Text Formatting

**Why isn't my bold text displaying correctly on mobile?**
Boldify uses Unicode mathematical characters to simulate bold. While most modern smartphones support them, some older browsers may display squares. Use the "Sans-Serif Bold" style for maximum compatibility.

**Can I mix multiple styles in a single post?**
Yes. Select different parts of your text in the editor and apply distinct styles (Bold, Italic, Script) to create visual hierarchy.

### LinkedIn & Networks

**Does using formatted text hurt my LinkedIn reach?**
LinkedIn does not directly penalize Unicode. However, screen readers may have difficulty with these characters. Use bold sparingly to highlight keywords, not entire paragraphs.

**How do I copy-paste without losing formatting?**
Use the built-in "Copy" button in Boldify. It captures the transformed Unicode text ready to paste directly into LinkedIn.

### MCP Configuration

**What is MCP and why use it?**
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) lets Boldify integrate with AI tools like Claude Desktop. Format text without leaving your usual writing interface.

**My MCP is not connecting. What should I do?**
Check that the path to the node executable is correct in your config file. Restart the desktop app after modifying claude_desktop_config.json.
